title: Malty Fluffy Kenyan Pap Porridge
description: A quick and comforting breakfast dish originating from Kenya, made with a bouquet garni and cooked in a clay pot for a unique flavor and texture. This tamasic-friendly breakfast option is perfect for a busy morning.

ingredients.name: Bouquet garni
ingredients.notes: Fresh herbs like thyme, rosemary, and parsley.

ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.notes: Grate the ginger before adding to the porridge.

instructions: Add water to the clay pot and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to medium and add maize meal, stirring constantly to prevent lumps.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add bouquet garni, coconut oil, salt, and black pepper to the porridge, stirring well to combine.
Continue to simmer for another 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the porridge has thickened and developed a malty flavor.
Stir in ghee or clarified butter, sesame oil, tahini, and grated ginger.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, until the porridge is smooth and creamy.
Remove the bouquet garni and serve the porridge hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Tahini can be omitted if you prefer a lighter porridge.
